metadata.working_title: Antipsychotic drugs selectively decorrelate long-range cortical interactions mediated by layer 5 intratelencephalic neurons

abstract_sketch.problem: Antipsychotic drugs are effective in treating positive symptoms of psychotic disorders, but the neural circuit-level changes underlying their therapeutic action across distributed cortex remain poorly understood.
abstract_sketch.gap: Prior work has localized dopamine D2 receptor antagonism at the synaptic and regional level, but has not linked antipsychotic action to layer- and cell-type-specific changes in inter-areal cortical coupling, which has been hypothesized to be altered in psychotic states.
abstract_sketch.approach: We used widefield calcium imaging across dorsal cortex in head-fixed mice navigating a virtual reality corridor, combined with cell-type-specific expression of GCaMP6 (C57BL/6 pan-neuronal via AAV-PHP.eB; Emx1-Cre for excitatory; Tlx3-Cre x Ai148 for layer 5 intratelencephalic (L5 IT); Cux2-CreERT2 x Ai148 for layer 2/3), and compared activity before and after single intraperitoneal injections of clozapine, aripiprazole, haloperidol, amphetamine, or saline.
abstract_sketch.key_results: Antipsychotics reduced both short- and long-range correlations in L5 IT neurons more strongly than in pan-neuronal or L2/3 populations (Tlx3 vs C57BL/6: short-range p<0.05, long-range p<10^-5; Cux2 vs Tlx3: short-range p<0.005, long-range p<10^-8, rank-sum). Decorrelation preferentially affected long-range pairs and was not mimicked by the psychostimulant amphetamine.
abstract_sketch.impact: These results identify L5 IT pyramidal neurons as a cell-type-specific substrate through which antipsychotic drugs reshape distributed cortical coupling, providing a circuit-level framework for therapeutic action and motivating layer-specific targets for next-generation compounds.

sections.title: Introduction
content_bullets: Distributed cortical interactions organize perception, action, and internal models; aberrant long-range coupling is implicated in psychotic disorders.
Antipsychotic drugs (clozapine, aripiprazole, haloperidol) act via monoaminergic receptors, but the cortical circuit mechanisms that translate receptor binding into therapeutic effect remain unclear.
Layer 5 intratelencephalic (L5 IT) pyramidal neurons are positioned to mediate long-range cortical coupling via dense inter-areal axonal projections.

We test the hypothesis that antipsychotics selectively alter correlated activity in L5 IT neurons, using cell-type-specific GCaMP6 expression and widefield imaging across dorsal cortex in behaving mice.
Predictive-processing framing of mismatch and locomotion-onset responses provides a behaviorally anchored readout of top-down versus bottom-up contributions to cortical activity.

Preview of findings: clozapine and other antipsychotics, but not amphetamine, reduce short- and long-range correlations most strongly in L5 IT neurons, consistent with a layer-specific decorrelation mechanism.

subsections.title: Widefield calcium imaging captures reproducible cortical activity during virtual navigation
content_bullets: Custom macroscope with 100 Hz sCMOS acquisition and 470 nm LED excitation through the intact cleared skull.
12 regions of interest covering dorsal cortex; closed-loop and open-loop locomotion onsets and visual flow onsets evoke structured responses.
Session exclusion criterion: activity > 30% DeltaF/F sustained >10 s excluded 10/3801 (0.26%) sessions.
Closed-loop and open-loop onset responses show genotype-dependent similarity (one-way ANOVA; Figure 1L).

subsections.title: Cell-type-specific GCaMP6 expression enables layer-resolved comparisons
content_bullets: Pan-neuronal expression via retro-orbital AAV-PHP.eB in 6 C57BL/6, 4 Emx1-Cre, and 4 Sst-Cre mice; transgenic Tlx3-Cre x Ai148 (L5 IT) and Cux2-CreERT2 x Ai148 (L2/3).
Mouse counts for onset response analyses: 6 C57BL/6, 4 Emx1-Cre, 7 Scnn1a-Cre, 14 Tlx3-Cre, 3 Ntsr1-Cre, 2 PV-Cre, 6 VIP-Cre, 6 Sst-Cre, 3 Cux2-CreERT2.
eGFP controls in Tlx3-Cre mice via AAV-PHP.eB-Ef1a-DIO-eGFP rule out hemodynamic contamination of calcium signals.
Contour maps of correlation versus distance at 50% of peak value provide a visual summary of spatial structure.

subsections.title: Clozapine modestly reduces cortical correlations when GCaMP is expressed pan-neuronally
content_bullets: C57BL/6 mice imaged before and after a single intraperitoneal injection of clozapine (4 mice; 0.2-10 ug/g body weight).
Correlation versus distance heatmaps (40 x 40 grid, gaussian-smoothed) before and after clozapine with matched contour lines.
Short-range and long-range correlations (split at 0.9 x bregma-lambda ~ 3.8 mm) show modest changes in pan-neuronal imaging.

subsections.title: Antipsychotics strongly and selectively decorrelate L5 IT activity
content_bullets: In Tlx3-Cre x Ai148 mice expressing GCaMP6 in L5 IT neurons, clozapine produces significant reductions in both short-range and long-range correlations (Figure 6; short-range: 182 pairs, long-range: 148 pairs; ***: p < 0.001).
Reduction is significantly larger than in pan-neuronal C57BL/6 mice (C57BL/6 vs Tlx3-Cre x Ai148, short-range: p < 0.05; long-range: p < 10^-5; rank-sum test).
Aripiprazole produces a qualitatively similar decorrelation pattern (3 Tlx3-Cre x Ai148 mice; Figures 7A-B).
Haloperidol reduces correlations in 3 Tlx3-Cre x Ai148 mice (short-range: 114 pairs, long-range: 84 pairs; *: p < 0.05, ***: p < 0.001).

subsections.title: Decorrelation is weaker in L2/3 (Cux2) neurons than in L5 IT (Tlx3) neurons
content_bullets: Cux2-CreERT2 x Ai148 mice (4 mice) treated with clozapine show a reduction in correlations that is weaker than that observed in Tlx3-Cre x Ai148 mice.
Cux2-CreERT2 vs Tlx3-Cre x Ai148: short-range p < 0.005, long-range p < 10^-8 (rank-sum), indicating layer-specific decorrelation.
Pairwise statistics: 137 short-range pairs, 127 long-range pairs for Cux2 clozapine comparison.

subsections.title: Psychostimulant amphetamine does not recapitulate antipsychotic decorrelation
content_bullets: Amphetamine (4 ug/g) in 3 Tlx3-Cre x Ai148 mice fails to produce the decorrelation seen with antipsychotics.
Dissociation between receptor-level pharmacology and cortical coupling effects implicates circuit-level, rather than purely dopaminergic, contributions.
Saline injection controls (0.9% NaCl) show no systematic change in correlations.

subsections.title: eGFP control confirms decorrelation is not a hemodynamic artifact
content_bullets: Tlx3-Cre mice retro-orbitally injected with AAV-PHP.eB-Ef1a-DIO-eGFP imaged before and after clozapine (6 mice).
Correlation-distance structure in eGFP signal does not mirror the GCaMP6 decorrelation, arguing that the effect reflects neural, not vascular, activity.
Isosbestic 405 nm illumination attempted but could not fully recover hemodynamic response; eGFP imaging provides the key control.

subsections.title: Long-range interactions are preferentially decorrelated
content_bullets: Quantification across antipsychotics shows that long-range pair reductions exceed short-range reductions in Tlx3 mice.
Pair counts for key comparisons: 204 short-range / 192 long-range pairs (pan-neuronal), 182 / 148 (Tlx3 clozapine), 114 / 84 (haloperidol/aripiprazole subsets).
Consistent with a cell-type-specific mechanism acting on distributed, inter-areal coupling rather than purely local circuits.

sections.title: Discussion
content_bullets: Summary: antipsychotics selectively decorrelate long-range L5 IT activity, a pattern not shared with amphetamine or L2/3 neurons.

Interpretation within predictive-processing frameworks: L5 IT neurons may broadcast internal model signals across cortex, and their decorrelation may underlie the calming of aberrant inter-areal coupling.
Relation to receptor pharmacology: clozapine, aripiprazole, and haloperidol differ in their D2/5-HT2A profiles but converge on L5 IT decorrelation, suggesting a common downstream circuit effect.
Limitations: widefield imaging samples predominantly superficial cortex (~100 um half-depth); absolute cell-count comparisons across genotypes are not possible; drug doses span a broad range.
Implications: cell-type- and layer-specific decorrelation provides a circuit-level biomarker for antipsychotic efficacy and motivates targeted pharmacology toward L5 IT circuits.

sections.title: Methods
content_bullets: Animals: C57BL/6, Emx1-Cre, Scnn1a-Cre, Tlx3-Cre x Ai148, Ntsr1-Cre, PV-Cre, VIP-Cre, Sst-Cre, Cux2-CreERT2 x Ai148 (tamoxifen 400 mg/kg food for >=1 week).
Surgery: fentanyl 0.05/midazolam 5/medetomidine 0.5 mg/kg anesthesia, antagonized with flumazenil 0.5 + atipamezole 2.5 mg/kg; analgesia buprenorphine 0.1 + Metacam 5 mg/kg.
Retro-orbital AAV-PHP.eB at >=10^13 GC/ml, 6 uL per eye.

Widefield imaging: custom macroscope with Nikon 85/1.8 and 50/1.4 objectives, 470 nm LED 90% duty cycle, SP490/LP490 filters, 525/50 emission, PCO edge 4.2 sCMOS, 100 Hz full speed (50 Hz for eGFP), 16-bit.
Drug doses (i.p.): clozapine 0.2-10 ug/g, aripiprazole 0.2 ug/g, haloperidol 0.1 ug/g, amphetamine 4 ug/g, saline undiluted.
Two-photon imaging: 12 kHz resonant scanner, Coherent Chameleon 930 nm, 15 Hz effective frame rate over 4 depths (15 um steps via P-726), 750x400 pixels over 350x350 um.
Signal processing: DeltaF/F0 with 8th-percentile 62.5 s window; ROIs = 12 regions on dorsal cortex; session exclusion at sustained >30% DeltaF/F for >10 s.
Correlation analysis: Pearson correlation across pairs of ROIs, split at 0.9 x bregma-lambda (~3.8 mm) into short/long range; heatmaps on 40 x 40 grid with gaussian smoothing; contour at 50% of peak.

Statistics: hierarchical bootstrap (1000 samples over mice then onsets) for time-course CIs; rank-sum test for correlation comparisons; family-wise error correction alpha_adj = alpha/m with m = 9 genotypes; Bonferroni for 9 comparisons.
